publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

origination.label: creator
origination.persname: Pell, Stuyvesant Morris, 1905-1943. (Title of work: Scribblings of an outdoor boy..)

did.abstract: Includes lab and lecture notes taken by Pell while at Cornell University, sketches and drawings, thesis and monographs.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Stuyvesant Morris Pell was a specialist in conservation of natural resources and manager of the 16,000 acre range of the Huron Mountain Club in northern Michigan. He was born in New York City and received a B.S. from Cornell in 1939 and an M.S. in 1941. For five years he was warden of the Pleasant Valley Bird and Wild Flower Sanctuary in Lenox, Massachusetts, and he spent one summer as a naturalist and ranger in Acadia National Park, Bar Harbor, Maine.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Includes lab and lecture notes taken by Pell while at Cornell University with sketches and drawings related to various courses; thesis, monographs, and notes on the common snapping turtle, ca.1939; notebooks with photographs and commentary on wildlife including a "Log of the Pleasant Valley Beaver Colony," 1932, "Log of the Survey of Southern Sanctuaries," 1937, and others from Yosemite Field School, 1939, and from Acadia National Park, Bar Harbor, Maine, 1940; broadsides, brochures, and pamphlets relating to wildlife sanctuaries; prepared biology slides, dissecting kit, and field magnifying lens; original manuscript, carbon copies, and galley proof of SCRIBBLINGS OF AN OUTDOOR BOY, an autobiography; sketches and sketchbooks, pen-and-ink drawings and original watercolor paintings, mostly of birds, wildlife, and camping scenes; also glass slides and photographs of Pell's watercolors, scenes, trips, and wildlife.

arrangement.head: SERIES LIST
arrangement.p: Series I. Student Papers Box 1 and Map case Coursework Lecture Notes Diplomas and Certificates
Series II. Field Work Boxes 1-2, 4 and Mapcase Folder 7 Field Notes and Notebooks Informational Materials
Series III. Manuscript and Reprints Boxes 3 and 4
Series V. Artwork Box 4, 9, 10 and Mapcase Folder 1-6 and 8-9
Series VI. Scientific and Art Objects Box 3 and 8

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Stuyvesant Morris Pell papers, #3459. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

Orithology.
Wildlife conservation.

controlaccess.corpname: Pleasant Valley Bird and Wild Flower Sanctuary

did.unittitle: Typed Paper: "An Analysis of the Food Habits and Preliminary Ecological Study of the Common Snapping Turtle"
Carbon copy of M.S. Thesis, "Notes on the Habits on the Common Snapping Turtle, Chelydra Serpentina (Linn.) in Central New York"
Turtle Problems - Notes, Articles and Correspondence
Thesis "Notes on the Habits on the Common Snapping Turtle, Chelydra Serpentina (Linn.) in Central New York"
Diploma from Cornell University, College of Agriculture, B.S.
"Log of the Pleasant Valley Beaver Colony" by S.M. Pell
Notes, scrapbook, check lists, talks at Yosemite Field School
Notes taken on Acadia National Park, Bar Harbor Maine

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pell had a temporary summer job here. Includes photographs.

did.unittitle: Log of the Survey of Southern Sanctuaries
2 small note books with bird records
Copy of "Report on Wildlife Studies at the Huron Mountain Club 1939-1942" by Richard Manville
Waterfowl Bulletins, U.S. Department of the Interior, Bureau of Biological Survey and Department of Agriculture

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bird Breeding in Several States, Canada and the West Indies. Conservation Legislation in Congress. Notes on plants and animal life

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Field Mice (Genus Microtus;) Their Economic Status and Their Natural Enemies" by S. Morris Pell. Notes, maps and articles.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also "Death and Transfiguration in Delaware" by William Vogt, National Association of Audubon Societies regarding mosquito control

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes fabric swatches for U.S. National Park Service Uniforms and Shirts

did.unittitle: 2 copies of Huron Mountain Club map, Marquette County, Michigan by S.M. Pell modified after
2 copies of Snipe Shooter's map of Shinnecock Bay, Long Island with annotations
6 posters advertising lectures by S. Morris Pell and 2 posters advertising the Pleasant Valley Bird and Wild Flower Sanctuary
Scribblings of an Outdoor Boy: Being the Reminiscence of Stuyvesant Morris Pell, Up to the Year 1943 when,‡Having Reached the Age of 38 Years He Came to a Great Adventure with Life

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, original typed manuscripts, 2 carbon copies, and galley proofs.

did.unittitle: Scribblings of an Outdoor Boy: Being the Reminiscence if Stuyvesant Morris Pell, Up to the Year 1943 when, Having Reached the Age of 38 Years He Came to a Great Adventure with Life
Wooden Box of 4"x3.25" glass plate slides

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 167 plants, birds, and countryside scenes. For both Box 5 and 6 many of these sides were issued by the Department of Public Education, Museum of Natural History (NYC). Others bear the name of Edward Van Altena, 71-79 W. 45th Street, NYC.

did.unittitle: Southern Trip - Birds, Great White Heron, Mallards
Trips - Cape May, Sweet Briar, and Yosemite
Negatives for Yosemite Valley Research Reserve
Sketch book of bird and camping scenes
23 watercolor paintings by Stuyvesant Morris Pell
"The Tale of Almeek the Beaver" by S. Morris Pell
55 Folio of watercolors and pencil drawings
17 Folio of charcoal drawings of animals
1 16"x11" pen and ink color wash of the skeleton of Nyroca vallisneria, with skull of Nyroca marila
2 pen and ink drawings of ducks
1 16"x11" pen and ink rendering of Procellariiform profiles (beaks)
14 15"x11" watercolor renderings of birds on boards
3 mounted watercolors of birds and 1 of horses
62 prints of pen and ink drawings of birds, waterfowl and hunting scenes
24 charcoal and crayon sketches of birds, fish etc. on brown paper
23 charcoal and crayon sketches of birds, fish etc. on brown paper
Series VI. Scientific and Art Objects
2 printing blocks for cover of "Bird Lore" designed by Pell
